title: Spear of Longinus behavior in End of Evangelion
display_title: Spear of Longinus behavior in End of Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Spear of Longinus behavior in End of Evangelion             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Mon Jul 23, 2012 6:20 pm I feel like I have a more than decent understanding of EOE after my four times watching it and all the material I've read on it. Even so, there's one event in the movie that doesn't sit right with me: As Eva-01 was breaking off its limiters at the beginning of Episode 26', the Spear of Longinus senses this from way up ON THE MOON and promptly zooms down to Earth in order to prevent it. This is expected of a device who's sole purpose is to subdue SOL or God-tier organisms. So why does it stop mere inches from Eva-01's throat, and then go on to play an essential role in the event that it was supposed to put a stop to? The only conclusion I can draw from this is that aiding the onset of Human Instrumentality was an intended purpose of the Spear. Any insight would be greatly appreciated.

[]: Re: Spear of Longinus behavior in End of Evangelion             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Jul 23, 2012 7:40 pm It's probably one of the most baffling apparent inconsistencies in the entirety of NGE. I've been struggling with it myself for years. Learning that the Spear was actually a security device intended to prevent what it ultimately seemed to help cause destroyed any kind of sense I had made at the time of its role in EoE. One possibility is that in its capacity as a "security device", its purpose is not only to prevent unwanted behavior by the Seeds but also facilitate desired behavior. The trick of it is that the line between the two can be very thin. For a while I have been wondering, for example, if part of what we saw in EoE was actually intended behavior by the FAR. After all, they went to all the trouble of creating a manual, the Secret Dead Sea Scrolls, for the purpose of helping the Lilin understand the Angels and the operation of the Spear as well as this "contest of survival". The Scrolls even go so far as to describe each of the 15 Adam-born Angels to some extent. Maybe the "contest of survival" is an intended part of LDO-ADO relations, with Instrumentality as the end goal, but the fact of the matter is that Instrumentality can be manipulated to produce ends that the FAR did not desire (such as the creation of a complemented god-being), and for that reason they made sure that Instrumentality required the security device so that it could also be there to try to prevent unwanted behavior. The end goal that the FAR might have intended for Instrumentality might have been something like what we saw in the Angel ending ("Good night") in NGE2, where, instead of complementation into a single being, the "winning side" gets to rebirth all souls from both sides as members of the winning race. (Maybe after some sleeping period and possibly also on another planet.) So in short, if Human Instrumentality had gone as the FAR had desired it, the result would have been to rebirth all humans and Angels as humans. And if Angel Instrumentality had occurred (as a result of an Angel getting into Lilith), then all humans and Angels would have been reborn (after who knows how long) as Angels. Either way, the conflict between these two seemingly irreconcilable races is resolved.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jul 23, 2012 9:59 pm Monk Ed: In Kaworu's PSP ending, it's hinted that the FAR's souls were collected into the seven Moons to begin with using a Third Impact-like event. Perhaps they envisioned this as being part of an ongoing human life cycle: use Seeds of Life (with their immortality and access to the Chamber of Guf) to move human souls from one planet to another, rebirthing them at each step. The FAR would want this cycle to continue, and, as such, would need subsequent generations of humans to know how to implement it. Seele, of course, had no intention of using the process for its actual purpose. Perhaps the Spears facilitated the original "FAR Impact" in some way, and, in EoE, the Spear expects that it'll be doing something similar -- helping to upload all of the human souls into a new Seed of Life (Eva-01), who'll then leave for a new world. According to NGE2, the Spear was supposed to be destroyed in order to complete Seele's objective, apparently because it posed a threat to the status quo of their new "god" (i.e., could "kill it"). For whatever it's worth, Eva-01 is Spear-less in the Seele ending, but has both the Spear and long tresses in the Everybody Gets Complemented ("Thank You") ending.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Jul 23, 2012 10:19 pm Reichu wrote: Monk Ed: In Kaworu's PSP ending, it's hinted that the FAR's souls were collected into the seven Moons to begin with using a Third Impact-like event. Perhaps they envisioned this as being part of an ongoing human life cycle: use Seeds of Life (with their immortality and access to the Chamber of Guf) to move human souls from one planet to another, rebirthing them at each step. The FAR would want this cycle to continue, and, as such, would need subsequent generations of humans to know how to implement it. Seele, of course, had no intention of using the process for its actual purpose. Perhaps the Spears facilitated the original "FAR Impact" in some way, and, in EoE, the Spear expects that it'll be doing something similar -- helping to upload all of the human souls into a new Seed of Life (Eva-01), who'll then leave for a new world.

[]:             Quote Post by Adam's wristwatch » Mon Jul 23, 2012 11:02 pm In the Dead Sea Scrolls, information regarding the seeds, moons and Lance was given. I thought that 3I was the complete undoing of creation. The fact that Seele could use the Lance at all, a security device, just points to that the human instrumentality project was hopelessly misguided.

[]: Re: Spear of Longinus behavior in End of Evangelion             Quote Post by Tribblepoo » Tue Jul 24, 2012 1:23 am riffraff11235 wrote: I feel like I have a more than decent understanding of EOE after my four times watching it and all the material I've read on it. Even so, there's one event in the movie that doesn't sit right with me: As Eva-01 was breaking off its limiters at the beginning of Episode 26', the Spear of Longinus senses this from way up ON THE MOON and promptly zooms down to Earth in order to prevent it. This is expected of a device who's sole purpose is to subdue SOL or God-tier organisms. So why does it stop mere inches from Eva-01's throat, and then go on to play an essential role in the event that it was supposed to put a stop to? The only conclusion I can draw from this is that aiding the onset of Human Instrumentality was an intended purpose of the Spear. Any insight would be greatly appreciated.

[]:             Quote Post by Nytloc » Tue Jul 24, 2012 3:43 am Am I the only person who thought that the Lance *attempted* to subdue Unit 1, but failed? I mean, look at it this way. The Lance is designed to protect its respective Seed of Life, and to prevent anyone from reaching Godhood. Normally I would assume it would kick in at any given time to prevent such an act from occuring, but in this scenario it was placed all the way up in lunar orbit, where it couldn't detect the God ascension scenario until it was too late. GNR forms, radiating immense energy, and the LoL can finally detect it from way out in space. It goes, "Oh crap," and flies in to try and stop it, but it's too late. The Lance can only lock down a single Seed of Life to prevent a God scenario from coming into fruition (as it did with Adam originally), but it can't touch something that's already obtained Godhood (which... logically, makes sense.) I simply think it was a crazy scenario that prevented the Lance from doing its job correctly. I never assumed there was anything else to it beyond that.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Jul 24, 2012 4:19 am Nytloc wrote: Am I the only person who thought that the Lance *attempted* to subdue Unit 1, but failed? I mean, look at it this way. The Lance is designed to protect its respective Seed of Life, and to prevent anyone from reaching Godhood. Normally I would assume it would kick in at any given time to prevent such an act from occuring, but in this scenario it was placed all the way up in lunar orbit, where it couldn't detect the God ascension scenario until it was too late. GNR forms, radiating immense energy, and the LoL can finally detect it from way out in space. It goes, "Oh crap," and flies in to try and stop it, but it's too late. The Lance can only lock down a single Seed of Life to prevent a God scenario from coming into fruition (as it did with Adam originally), but it can't touch something that's already obtained Godhood (which... logically, makes sense.) I simply think it was a crazy scenario that prevented the Lance from doing its job correctly. I never assumed there was anything else to it beyond that.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jul 24, 2012 6:39 am Monk Ed wrote: that much is evidenced by how it turns Eva-01 into the "well of souls" as described by Fuyutsuki.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Jul 24, 2012 6:47 am Reichu wrote: "Well of souls" was bochan_bird's rendition of yorishiro . Seele were putting Eva-01 into this position by having the harpies "crucify" her (i.e., give her artificial Doors of Guf?). The Spear of Longinus (Original) was just floating around while this was happening.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jul 24, 2012 6:57 am Monk Ed wrote: I was thinking of this part specifically

[]:             Quote Post by Archont » Tue Jul 24, 2012 7:00 am Nytloc wrote: Am I the only person who thought that the Lance *attempted* to subdue Unit 1, but failed?

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Jul 24, 2012 7:13 am Reichu wrote: In that case, the actual phrase Fuyutsuki says doesn't have anything to do with souls. Bochan's translation is a bit misleading. See here (and proceed to symbv's subsequent post).

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Tue Jul 24, 2012 12:41 pm Nytloc wrote: The Lance can only lock down a single Seed of Life to prevent a God scenario from coming into fruition (as it did with Adam originally), but it can't touch something that's already obtained Godhood (which... logically, makes sense.)

[]:             Quote Post by Tribblepoo » Tue Jul 24, 2012 6:16 pm Monk Ed wrote: Maybe it's understandable why bochan made that translation error, because when you take it all together in context, it's almost like the Tree of Life is the well of souls is the embryo of life all at the same time.

[]: Re: Spear of Longinus behavior in End of Evangelion             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Jul 24, 2012 6:53 pm riffraff11235 wrote: Any insight would be greatly appreciated.

[]: Re: Spear of Longinus behavior in End of Evangelion             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Jul 24, 2012 7:40 pm Lists? I love lists! Bagheera wrote: 1. The LoL does not appear to be necessary for SEELE's version of Instrumentality, as they were clearly ready to proceed without it.

[]: Re: Spear of Longinus behavior in End of Evangelion             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Jul 24, 2012 7:46 pm Monk Ed wrote: That's at least possible with the plan they ended up using, although they seemed to think they needed it to use Lilith, and Gendo throwing it away is what forced them to resort to using Eva-01. But even with Eva-01, I find it suspect that they say the line, "To restore the Tree of Life!" and then later the Spear comes flying back home from the moon and merges with Eva-01 to form something which Fuyutsuki calls the Tree of Life, as if Seele really did need the Spear the whole time but had no way of summoning it back without using Eva-01.

[]: Re: Spear of Longinus behavior in End of Evangelion             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jul 24, 2012 10:33 pm Bagheera wrote: I do not consider that related to Impact, for the reasons stated above. It's all about Yui's transformation; Impact/Instrumentality is a completely separate (and unrelated, and ultimately irrelevant from the Lance's PoV) event.

[]: Re: Spear of Longinus behavior in End of Evangelion             Quote Post by Tribblepoo » Tue Jul 24, 2012 11:02 pm Reichu wrote: Well, consider what was forwarded previously: that Third Impact is directly utilizing the FAR's procedure for preparing a Seed for her transgalactic voyage. Reducing humans to souls and uploading them into a Seed's Chamber of Guf is intrinsically for the purpose of making them easily transportable. Turning Yui into a Seed is in no way separable from the rest of the Impact; it's all intimately connected.
